prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_DWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=2.06) and high seasonality variance (0.72), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_svr, FT_svr) differ from late horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, ONLY_CWT_catboost). The trend champion ONLY_DWT_catboost has the lowest trend slope error, while ARIMA best captures seasonality. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_DWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_FT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_FT_svr", "late": "CWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_FT_svr", "FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "DWT_catboost", "FT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["CWT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ARIMA", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "catboost", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 2.0564,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=2.06) and high seasonality variance (0.72), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_svr, FT_svr) differ from late horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, ONLY_CWT_catboost). The trend champion ONLY_DWT_catboost has the lowest trend slope error, while ARIMA best captures seasonality. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.949 (≥0.25 threshold) justifies robust combination like dba_combination. RMSE spread ratio=2.06 shows extreme model dispersion, favoring weighted approaches with shrinkage. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends 'dba_combination' for high seasonality variance (0.72). topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 adds selection diversity while inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 provides adaptive weighting with shrinkage=0.35 to mitigate overfitting on only 3 windows."
}

Score margin between top2 is small (0.0046) indicating competitive set.",

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=2.06) and high seasonality variance (0.72), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_svr, FT_svr) differ from late horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, ONLY_CWT_catboost). The trend champion ONLY_DWT_catboost has the lowest trend slope error, while ARIMA best captures seasonality. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.949 (≥0.25 threshold) justifies robust combination like dba_combination. RMSE spread ratio=2.06 shows extreme model dispersion, favoring weighted approaches with shrinkage. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends 'dba_combination' for high seasonality variance (0.72). topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 adds selection diversity while inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 provides adaptive weighting with shrinkage=0.35 to mitigate overfitting on only 3 windows.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k5, best_per_horizon_by_validation), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). All candidates are valid and from current_candidate_names. n_windows=3 with anti-leakage rolling/expanding mode - no leakage detected. Universe leaderboards show top performers but current set already includes strong candidates. No pruning needed as we have only 4 candidates (no single type >=4). Score margin between top2 is small (0.0046) indicating competitive set.
